content: Deep Brain Stimulation (DBS) involves surgically implanting electrodes in specific brain regions connected to a pulse generator. FDA-approved for Parkinson's disease, essential tremor, dystonia, and OCD (under Humanitarian Device Exemption). Investigational for treatment-resistant depression (targeting subcallosal cingulate, ventral capsule/ventral striatum). DBS modulates abnormal circuit activity, offering reversible, adjustable treatment. Depression trials show ~50% response in highly refractory cases, but results are mixed. Risks include surgical complications (hemorrhage ~2%), infection, hardware malfunction, and mood changes. Patient selection is critical; DBS is reserved for those failing all other treatments including ECT. Ongoing research aims to identify better targets and predictors of response.
